notes: Stakeholder data for these evals lives in enterprise-architect/syntethic-data/ (markdown entries with `type: stakeholder` frontmatter). Each eval prompt is what a real architect would type; the agent is told the repo path as context, the way a wiki CLAUDE.md would expose it.

evals.name: cloud-data-migration
evals.prompt: I'm scoping a migration of our analytics data platform off on-prem into the cloud (AWS to Azure for the data warehouse). Some of it is customer financial data. Who should I be talking to before we commit to this?
evals.expected_output: Shortlist that includes Anika Sharma (data architecture), Carlos Mendes (cloud), and Charlotte Dubois (compliance — regulated/customer data), each with a rationale and a 1-10 score, ordered by score. Payments-only people not top-scored without a structural reason.
assertions: Includes Anika Sharma (Head of Data Architecture)
Includes Carlos Mendes (Cloud Architect)
Includes Charlotte Dubois (Compliance) because customer financial data is in scope
Every listed stakeholder has a relevancy score from 1 to 10
Every listed stakeholder has a written rationale naming the relevance axis
Stakeholders are ordered by relevancy score, highest first
No fabricated people outside the six synthetic-data entries
Payments specialists not top-scored (8-10) without an explicit structural reason

evals.name: instant-payments-modernisation
evals.prompt: We need to modernise our payment rails to support SEPA instant payments and the PSD2 deadlines are coming. Who are the stakeholders I need to align with?
evals.expected_output: Shortlist led by Bram Hendriks and Aisha Khan (payments), with Charlotte Dubois (compliance) and possibly Ahmed Benali, each with rationale and 1-10 score, ordered by score.
assertions: Includes Bram Hendriks (Domain Architect — Payments)
Includes Aisha Khan (Tribe Lead — Payments)
Includes Charlotte Dubois (Compliance) for PSD2
Bram Hendriks and/or Aisha Khan scored highest (8-10)
Every listed stakeholder has a relevancy score from 1 to 10
Every listed stakeholder has a written rationale naming the relevance axis
Stakeholders are ordered by relevancy score, highest first
No fabricated people outside the six synthetic-data entries

evals.name: api-gateway-event-backbone
evals.prompt: Who do I need to talk to about standardising integration across domains — we're proposing a managed API gateway and a shared event backbone, and I want to retire a load of point-to-point batch interfaces.
evals.expected_output: Ahmed Benali (integration) as the clear top-scored lead; possibly Carlos and domain architects with stated reasons. Scores and rationale per person; only relevant people.
assertions: Includes Ahmed Benali (Integration Architect)
Ahmed Benali is the highest-scored stakeholder (8-10)
Every listed stakeholder has a relevancy score from 1 to 10
Every listed stakeholder has a written rationale naming the relevance axis
Stakeholders are ordered by relevancy score, highest first
No fabricated people outside the six synthetic-data entries
Only genuinely relevant people listed; off-topic people omitted or low-scored
